type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
All of the digester gas produced at Pine Creek was burned in Thermal Oxidizers, which converted H2S in the digester gas into SO2, and subsequently SO2 was released into the atmosphere.

Total digester gas production in the plant = 4,187,146 m3
Typical H2S content in the digester gas = 0.20% (v/v)

When H2S is burned, stoichiometrically 34 tonnes of H2S is converted into 64 tonnes of SO2.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This is due to increase in the annual average ammonia-nitrogen concentration in the final effluent from 0.25 mg/L to 1.22 mg/L as well as increase in the annual total flow by 7.8%.

The decrease/increase from previous year is considered to be within normal variation for the biological treatment processes used. All discharges were within Provincial Approval limits.

The uncontrolled air-emission factor for Publicly-owned Treatment Works = 2.2 kg/1000 m3 wastewater treated (Battye et al., 1994) was used to estimate ammonia air release from the municipal WWTP. (refer: Appendix D, Table D-22 in NPRI Guidance Manual for the Wastewater Sector)

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: Using the mass balance approach, the estimated P quantity released in the biosolids is calculated as incoming (influent) P minus outgoing (effluent) P.  The estimated P quantity released in the biosolids is not of the highest certainty and should only be taken as a general indicator of the magnitude of the quantity released.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: The decrease/increase from previous year is considered to be within normal variation for the biological treatment processes used. All discharges were within Provincial Approval limits.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Emission Factor “1.066 kgVOCs/1000 m3 wastewater processed" was used to determine the Total VOCs air-emission. Pine Creek Plant commissioned the anaerobic digesters in November 2009.The VOCs contained in the digester gas are burned off along with digester gas in the thermal oxidizers. Using the emission factors available from FIRE 6.25 database, these individual VOC compounds emitted from digesters were estimated and then subtracted from ‘Total VOCs’ in order to estimate the net VOCs emission to the atmosphere.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment:
Although the plant met 10-tonne Total VOCs threshold under Part 4, our VOCs air-emission model based on the Emission Factors (from EPA FIRE 6.25 database) determined that none of the 75 speciated VOCs individually met the 1-tonne threshold criterion for the Part 5 Substances reporting.

The decrease/increase from previous year is considered to be within normal variation for the biological treatment processes used.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This is due to the increased digester biogas production this year (3,915,155 m3) compared to the last year (2,063,154 m3).

All of the digester gas produced at Pine Creek was burned in Thermal Oxidizers, which converted H2S in the digester gas into SO2, and subsequently SO2 was released into the atmosphere.

Total digester gas production in the plant = 3,915,155 m3
Typical H2S content in the digester gas = 0.20% (v/v)

When H2S is burned, stoichiometrically 34 tonnes of H2S is converted into 64 tonnes of SO2.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: The decrease/increase from previous year is considered to be within normal variation for the biological treatment processes used. All discharges were within Provincial Approval limits.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
In 2013, we did not consider P emission in the air as it is believed that all of the P is either released in water or biosolids.  Hence, the P quantity released in the water is lower this year.

This is due to increase in the annual average total phosphorus concentration in the final effluent from 0.10 mg/L to 0.15 mg/L as well as increase in the annual total flow by 17.5%

The decrease/increase from previous year is considered to be within normal variation for the biological treatment processes used. All discharges were within Provincial Approval limits.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
This is due to increase in the amount of the digested sludge transferred to the Shepard Lagoons compared to the previous year.

Using the mass balance approach, the estimated P quantity released in the biosolids is calculated as incoming (influent) P minus outgoing (effluent) P.  The estimated P quantity released in the biosolids is not of the highest certainty and should only be taken as a general indicator of the magnitude of the quantity released.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: The decrease/increase from previous year is considered to be within normal variation for the biological treatment processes used. All discharges were within Provincial Approval limits.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This is due to increase in the annual total flow by +17.5%.

Emission Factor “1.066 kgVOCs/1000 m3 wastewater processed" was used to determine the Total VOCs air-emission. Pine Creek Plant commissioned the anaerobic digesters in November 2009.The VOCs contained in the digester gas are burned off along with digester gas in the thermal oxidizers. Using the emission factors available from FIRE 6.25 database, these individual VOC compounds emitted from digesters were estimated and then subtracted from ‘Total VOCs’ in order to estimate the net VOCs emission to the atmosphere.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment:
Although the plant met 10-tonne Total VOCs threshold under Part 4, our VOCs air-emission model based on the Emission Factors (from EPA FIRE 6.25 database) determined that none of the 75 speciated VOCs individually met the 1-tonne threshold criterion for the Part 5 Substances reporting.

The decrease/increase from previous year is considered to be within normal variation for the biological treatment processes used.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The digester biogas production this year decreased from the last year’s 2,403,406 m3 to 2,063,155 m3.

All the digester gas produced at Pine Creek was burned in the Thermal Oxidizers, which converted H2S in digester gas into SO2, and subsequently the SO2 released to atmosphere.

Total digester gas production in the plant = 2,063,155 m3
Typical H2S content in the digester gas = 0.20% (v/v)

When H2S is burned, stoichiometrically 34 tonnes of H2S is converted into 64 tonnes of SO2.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The increase in the reported quantity from previous year is due to increase in the Cd concentration of the final effluent (from 2011’s 0.688ug Cd/L  to 0.938ug Cd/L).

A municipal wastewater treatment plant has little control over amounts of Cd contained in the influent wastewater. As such, the Cadmium entering the plant leaves the plant in one way or another.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: The increase from previous year is due to the increased amount of digested sludge transferred to the Shepard Lagoons. (from 2011’s 2,118 dry tonnes @ 1.67 mg Cd /kg of dry solids to 3,139 dry tonnes @ 1.72 mg Cd/kg of dry solids). Proportionately, the Cadmium quantity contained in the liquid phase of the digested sludge also increased.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The increase in the reported quantity from previous year is due to increase in the Pb concentration of the final effluent (from 2011’s 0.745 ug Pb/L  to 0.938 ug Pb/L).

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: The increase from previous year is due to the increased amount of digested sludge transferred to the Shepard Lagoons as well as the Pb concentration increase (from 2011’s  2,118 dry tonnes @ 36.55 mg Pb/kg of dry solids to  3,139 dry tonnes @ 40.09 mg Pb/kg of dry solids). Proportionately, the Lead quantity contained in the liquid phase of the digested sludge also increased.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: The increase from previous year is due to the increased amount of digested sludge transferred to the Shepard Lagoons. (from 2011’s 2,118 dry tonnes @ 1.28 mg Hg /kg of dry solids to 3,139 dry tonnes @ 1.19 mg Hg/kg of dry solids). Proportionately, the Mercury quantity contained in the liquid phase of the digested sludge also increased.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The decrease from previous year is due to the increases in total annual volume of wastewater treated at the Plant and annual average ammonia-nitrogen concentration of the final effluent (from 2011’s 26,803 ML @ 0.85 mg/L to 25,838 ML @ 0.21 mg/L).

The uncontrolled air-emission factor for Publicly-owned Treatment Works = 2.2 kg/1000 m3 wastewater treated (Battye et al., 1994) was used to estimate ammonia air release from the municipal WWTP. (refer: Appendix D, Table D-22 in NPRI Guidance Manual for the Wastewater Sector)

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: The increase from previous year is due to the increased amount of digested sludge transferred to the Shepard Lagoons. (from 2011’s 2,118 dry tonnes @ 2.951% NH3 of dry solids to 3,139 dry tonnes @ 2.805% NH3 of dry solids). Proportionately, the ammonia contained in the liquid phase of the digested sludge also increased.

substance.en: Nitrate ion in solution at pH >= 6.0
substance.fr: Nitrate (ion en sol. à un pH de >= 6.0)

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The Total P concentration in the final effluent decreased from 0.21 mg/L (in 2011) to 0.11 mg/L this year.

Using the mass balance approach, the estimated P quantity released to the atmosphere is calculated as incoming P in the influent minus outgoing P in the effluent & biosolids. In 2012, this quantity appeared as negative and thus the estimated P quantity released to the air is not reported, whereas last year the value was positive and therefore was reported.

The decrease/increase from previous year is considered to be within normal variation for the biological treatment processes used. All discharges were within Provincial Approval limits.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: The increase from the previous year is due to the increased amount of digested sludge transferred to the Shepard Lagoons. (From 2011’s 2,118 dry tonnes @ 4.29%P of dry solids to 3,139 dry tonnes @ 4.07%P of dry solids). Proportionately, the phosphorus contained in the liquid phase of the digested sludge also increased.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Emission Factor “1.066 kgVOCs/1000 m3 wastewater processed" was used to determine the Total VOCs air-emission. Pine Creek Plant commissioned the anaerobic digesters in November 2009.The VOCs contained in the digester gas are burned off along with digester gas in the thermal oxidizers. Using the emission factors available from FIRE 6.25 database, these individual VOC compounds emitted from digesters were estimated and then subtracted from ‘Total VOCs’ in order to estimate the net VOCs emission to the atmosphere.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: Although the plant met 10-tonne Total VOCs threshold under Part 4, our VOCs air-emission model based on the Emission Factors (from EPA FIRE 6.25 database) determined that none of the 75 speciated VOCs individually met the 1-tonne threshold criterion for the Part 5 Substances reporting.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
All the digester gas produced at Pine Creek was burned in the Thermal Oxidizers, which converted H2S in digester gas into SO2, and subsequently the SO2 released to atmosphere.

Total digester gas production in the plant = 2,403,406 m3
Typical H2S content in the digester gas = 0.20% (v/v)

When H2S is burned, stoichiometrically 34 tonnes of H2S is converted into 64 tonnes of SO2.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: This year Cd MDL = 2 ug/L compared to last year’s 1 ug/L. From June – Dec 2011, the effluent Cd concentrations detected were below MDL of 2 ug/L. However, for estimation purpose of Cd loading in the effluent, ½ MDL = 1 ug/L was used for June to Dec, resulting in much higher estimated Cd loading in the effluent compared to the last year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: The decrease from previous year is due to the decreased Cd concentration in the digested solids sludge transferred to the Shepard Lagoons (from 2010’s 2.203 mg Cd /kg of dry solids to 1.67 mg Cd/kg of dry solids).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: This year Pb MDL = 2 ug/L compared to last year’s 0.5 ug/L. From June – Dec 2011, the effluent Pb concentrations detected were below MDL of 2 ug/L. However, for estimation purpose of Pb loading in the effluent, ½ MDL = 1 ug/L was used for June - Dec, resulting in much higher estimated Pb loading in the effluent compared to last year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: The decrease from previous year is due to the reduced amount of digested sludge transferred to the Shepard Lagoons as well as the Pb concentration decrease (from 2010’s  2,429 dry tonnes @ 35.59 mg Pb/kg of dry solids to  2,118 dry tonnes @ 36.55 mg Pb/kg of dry solids).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The increase from previous year is due to the total wastewater volume treated at the Plant increasing by 2 ML/d (from 2010’s 24,334 ML @ 0.168 ug Hg/L  to 26,803 ML @ 0.168 ug Hg/L).

There was no data for effluent Hg concentrations in 2007. Therefore, the amount reported is based on the average Hg concentration for years 2000-2003. As such, the reported data is not of the highest certainty and should only be taken as a general indicator of the magnitude of the quantity released.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: The decrease from previous year is due to the decreased Hg concentration in the digested solids sludge transferred to the Shepard Lagoons (from 2010’s 1.60 mg Cd /kg of dry solids to 1.28 mg Hg/kg of dry solids).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The decrease or increase from previous year is considered to be within normal variation for the biological treatment process used. All discharges were within Provincial Approval limits.

The increase from previous year is due to the increases in total annual volume of wastewater treated at the Plant and annual average ammonia-nitrogen concentration of the final effluent (from 2010’s 24,334 ML @ 0.23 mg/L to 26,803 ML @ 0.0.85 mg/L).

The uncontrolled air-emission factor for Publicly-owned Treatment Works = 2.2 kg/1000 m3 wastewater treated (Battye et al., 1994) was used to estimate ammonia air release from the municipal WWTP. (refer: Appendix D, Table D-22 in NPRI Guidance Manual for the Wastewater Sector)

substance.en: Nitrate ion in solution at pH >= 6.0
substance.fr: Nitrate (ion en sol. à un pH de >= 6.0)

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The decrease from previous year is mainly due to decrease in concentration of nitrate-nitrogen concentration of the final effluent although the total wastewater volume treated at the Plant increased by 2 ML/d (from 2010’s 24,334 ML @ 7.82 mg/L to 26803 ML @ 6.14 mg/L).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The decrease or increase from previous year is considered to be within normal variation for the biological treatment processes used. All discharges were within Provincial Approval limits.

Using the mass balance approach, the estimated quantity released to air appears to be higher than last year. However, this estimated air release quantity data is not of the highest certainty and should only be taken to be a general indicator of the magnitude of the quantity released. Also, the TP concentration in the final effluent increased from 0.13 mg/L (in 2010) to 0.21 mg/L this year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: The decrease from previous year is due to the decreased amount of digested sludge transferred to the Shepard Lagoons. (from 2010’s 2,429 dry tonnes @ 4.39%P of dry solids to 2,118 dry tonnes @ 4.29%P of dry solids). Proportionately, the phosphorus contained in the liquid phase of the digested sludge also decreased.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The increase from previous year is due to the increased wastewater volume treated at the Plant (from 2010’s 24,334 ML to 26,803 ML).


Emission Factor “1.066 kg VOCs/1000 m3 wastewater processed" was used to determine the Total VOCs air-emission. Pine Creek Plant commissioned the anaerobic digesters in November 2009.The VOCs contained in the digester gas are burned off along with digester gas in the thermal oxidizers. Using the emission factors available from FIRE 6.25 database, these individual VOC compounds emitted from digesters were estimated and then subtracted from ‘Total VOCs’ in order to estimate the net VOCs emission to the atmosphere.

Although the plant met 10-tonne Total VOCs threshold under Part 4, our VOCs air-emission model based on the Emission Factors (from EPA FIRE 6.25 database) determined that none of the 75 speciated VOCs individually met the 1-tonne threshold criterion for the Part 5 Substances reporting.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: A municipal wastewater treatment plant does not have control on how much cadmium is received in its influent. The plant does not treat for cadmium and therefore cadmium received at the plant leaves the plant in one way or another with little control over its effluent concentrations.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Previous year (2018) recorded all samples below MDL hence release assumed to be zero.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: A municipal wastewater treatment plant does not have control on how much lead is received in its influent. The plant does not treat for lead and therefore lead received at the plant leaves the plant in one way or another with little control over its effluent concentrations.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: This increase/decrease is considered within normal operation of a biological nutrient removal wastewater treatment plant.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: In 2019 the method was changed to direct testing for better accuracy. The testing started in September so for previous 8 months, assumed the highest recorded concentration to provide a conservative estimate. type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: This WWTP removes Ammonia/Nitrogen to meet the effluent quality required in our Provincial and Federal regulations. In addition to having no control over the influent (quality or quantity), Ammonia/Nitrogen concentration and volume in the effluent pathways (air, plant effluent and biosolids) varies each year due to population changes, industrial activity, and plant performance. As a result, the quantity varies each year.

substance.en: Nitrate ion in solution at pH >= 6.0
substance.fr: Nitrate (ion en sol. à un pH de >= 6.0)

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: This WWTP removes Total Nitrogen to meet the effluent quality required in our regulations. In addition to having no control over the influent (quality or quantity), Nitrate/Nitrogen concentration and volume in the plant effluent varies each year due to population changes, industrial activity, and plant performance. As a result, the on-site release quantity varies each year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: This WWTP removes Total Phosphorus to meet the effluent quality required in our Provincial and Federal regulations. However, in addition to having no control over the influent (quality or quantity), the Phosphorus concentration and volume in the effluent pathways (plant effluent and biosolids) varies each year due to population changes, industrial activity, and plant performance. As a result, the quantity varies each year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: PM10 emission is caused due to combustion of fuel (digester gas, natural gas or diesel) and its rate of consumption varies each year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: PM2.5 emission is caused due to combustion of fuel (digester gas, natural gas or diesel) and its rate of consumption varies each year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: VOC emission is caused due to combustion of fuel (digester gas, natural gas or diesel) and its rate of consumption varies each year. It also is a factor of the influent wastewater which also varies each year.

type.fr: Nature des autres rejets non ponctuels

comments.comment: Substances are released in different process areas. Areas determined from published emission factors
